mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP gradient intercepts the 4-byte DeviceClass integer compare and preserves inputs whose field is Hamming-closer to 'scnr', ratcheting from 'mntr' toward the target literal byte by byte.
one_line: VP CMP_MAP gradient walks 4-byte DeviceClass toward 'scnr' FOURCC

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP distance-bucket feedback gives value_profile an operand-distance gradient that incrementally drives TagCount upward across the MAX_TABLE_TAG threshold; naive's edge-only feedback provides no such gradient.
one_line: VP gradient walks 4-byte TagCount past MAX_TABLE_TAG threshold

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP distance feedback over the multi-byte '<![CDATA[' opener comparisons preserves inputs that partially match the literal, giving value_profile a gradient toward assembling the opener; naive gets no gradient and rarely lands the full literal plus the required truncated content shape.
one_line: VP gradient incrementally assembles '<![CDATA[' opener from partial matches

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP distance-bucket feedback rewards progressive prefix matches on tokenizer/keyword comparisons, preserving partial-progress seeds that spell '>>' operator runs; naive's plain edge feedback cannot steer toward the parseable shift expression.
one_line: VP CMP_MAP gradient assembles '>>' shift expression to reach OP_ShiftRight

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP gradient over integer comparison callbacks rewards structured SQL-plus-operator inputs that produce the token class driving the GetToken true branch; naive's edge-only feedback offers no such gradient and its seeds are malformed byte soup that never form coherent SELECT/operator tokens.
one_line: VP CMP gradient steers toward structured SQL tokens driving GetToken true branch

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP intercepts tokenizer/parser character comparisons against operator-token codepoints and writes distance-bucket entries; inputs progressively closer to forming '>=' in a comparison context are rewarded with new buckets, giving a gradient toward TK_GE that naive cannot follow.
one_line: VP CMP_MAP gradient assembles '>=' operator token to reach TK_GE switch case

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP intercepts the tokenizer's character comparison against '=', preserving inputs whose byte is operand-closer to '=' than any seen before, climbing the gradient until '=' lands immediately after '>'/'<' to form '>=' or '<=' that naive cannot steer toward.
one_line: VP CMP_MAP gradient steers second character to '=' to form two-char comparison operator
